alignments.preprint_claim_text: Rotation based double random forests result in better diversity among the base learners and hence, better generalization performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed rotation based double random forests improve generalization performance by enhancing diversity among base learners through different transformations at each node.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result but adds the mechanism ('different transformations at each node'), clarifying how diversity is achieved while keeping the original claim's intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Oblique double random forest models are multivariate decision trees that capture the geometric properties of the data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The oblique double random forest captures geometric properties of the data and grows decision trees of sufficient depth, leading to better generalization performance.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the geometric-capture claim but extends it to note deeper trees and better generalization, broadening the original contribution with an explicit performance implication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The standard random forest may not grow sufficiently large decision trees, resulting in suboptimal performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed ensembles of decision trees produce larger trees compared to standard ensembles, resulting in improved performance due to the use of bagging at each non-leaf node.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the limitation of standard forests by contrasting them with the proposed ensembles and gives a mechanistic explanation (bagging), clarifying the original limitation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Different regularization techniques are employed in oblique double random forest to tackle small sample size problems.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not mention regularization for small-sample problems, so this preprint claim is omitted in the published claim set.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Statistical analysis and experimental results indicate that the proposed oblique and rotation double random forest models are more effective than baseline models on benchmark datasets.
alignments.rationale: This comparative experimental-result claim appears in the publication but has no counterpart in the preprint's claim list; it is an added result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Quantifying the uncertainty of supervised learning models is essential for making more reliable predictions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Quantifying the uncertainty of supervised learning models is essential for making more reliable predictions.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim repeated in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Epistemic uncertainty can be reduced by collecting more data or refining the learning models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Epistemic uncertainty can be reduced by collecting more data or refining the learning models.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim repeated in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper introduces a hierarchical categorization of epistemic uncertainty learning techniques along with their representative models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper introduces a hierarchical categorization of epistemic uncertainty learning techniques along with their representative models.
alignments.rationale: Identical contribution claim present in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The review covers epistemic uncertainty learning techniques in supervised learning over the last five years.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The review covers epistemic uncertainty learning techniques in supervised learning over the last five years.
alignments.rationale: Identical coverage claim present in both preprint and publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper discusses research gaps and possible future research directions in the field of epistemic uncertainty.
alignments.rationale: This discussion of gaps and directions appears in the publication but was not listed among the preprint's explicit claims, so it is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed IQNC-M framework can effectively handle various extreme events, such as traffic state perturbations, service interruptions, and demand surges.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed IQNC-M framework can effectively handle general perturbations and various extreme events, such as traffic state perturbations and demand surges.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same capability with minor wording changes; intent and scope remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The IQNC-M framework achieves efficient and reliable control decisions through better handling of uncertainties/events in real-time transit operations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The IQNC-M framework improves both efficiency and reliability of the transit system compared to traditional holding control models and state-of-the-art MARL models.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the original efficiency/reliability claim explicit and comparative against baselines, broadening the scope to show empirical improvement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An interpretable meta-learning module incorporated into the distributional MARL framework is an effective solution to circumvent the credit assignment issue in the transit system.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The interpretable meta-learning module incorporated into the distributional MARL framework is an effective solution to circumvent the credit assignment issue in the transit system.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this contribution/conclusion verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study integrates implicit quantile network and meta-learning to develop a distributional MARL framework for continuous control.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of implicit quantile network and meta-learning in the IQNC-M framework leads to efficient and reliable control decisions in real-time transit operations.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's contribution (integration) is expressed in the publication as leading to concrete efficient/reliable decisions, clarifying its practical effect.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Long-term motion patterns play a pivotal role in recognizing an event in video surveillance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Long-term motion patterns play a pivotal role in recognizing an event in video surveillance.
alignments.rationale: Identical high-level result in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The hybrid Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) + Recurrent Neural Network (RNN) architecture significantly exploits temporal features for event recognition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The hybrid CNN + RNN architecture significantly outperforms standard temporal CNNs in event recognition.
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the original claim by claiming actual outperforming of standard temporal CNNs (stronger performance assertion), broadening scope and amplifying confidence in effectiveness.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed multi-tier fusion strategy improves precision in classification compared to state-of-the-art methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The multi-tier fusion strategy enhances precision in classification compared to state-of-the-art methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the same comparative precision-improvement claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Focusing on better classification of video sequences leads to robust actuation of systems designed for event surveillance and activity tracking.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Focusing on better classification of video sequences leads to robust actuation of systems for event surveillance and activity tracking.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about downstream system robustness is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RL agents initialized with random matrices reach the optimal SR place field faster and showcase a quicker reduction in value error.
alignments.pub_claim_text: RL agents initialized with random matrices reach the optimal SR place field faster and showcase a quicker reduction in value error.
alignments.rationale: Claim is carried over identically to the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Random agents exhibit a faster decrease in step length across larger grid-world environments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Random agents exhibit a faster decrease in step length across larger grid-world environments.
alignments.rationale: Identical empirical result in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides insights into the neurobiological interpretations of these results and their implications for understanding intelligence.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings provide insights into the neurobiological interpretations of learning efficiency in RL agents.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the neurobiological-interpretation insight with slightly different wording but same intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These findings could have profound implications for the field of artificial intelligence, particularly in the design of learning algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: These findings could have profound implications for the field of artificial intelligence, particularly in the design of learning algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The implication-for-AI claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method is able to pour granular materials into the desired configuration both in simulated and real scenarios. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method is able to pour granular materials into the desired configuration both in simulated and real scenarios. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Text is essentially identical between preprint and publication; the result claim is retained without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A graph-based representation is proposed to model the interaction dynamics of granular materials and rigid bodies manipulating them. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A graph-based representation is proposed to model the interaction dynamics of granular materials and rigid bodies manipulating them. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution wording is the same in both versions and retained verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The manipulation trajectories are planned by minimising the Wasserstein distance between a predicted distribution of granular particles and their desired configuration. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The manipulation trajectories are planned by minimising the Wasserstein distance between a predicted distribution of granular particles and their desired configuration. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The planning-by-Wasserstein claim appears unchanged in substance; only label (conclusion/result) differs but content is the same.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The method uses a graph neural network (GNN) to model particle interactions via message-passing. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit implementation detail (use of a GNN/message-passing) that was not stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed architecture based on racetrack memory (RTM) accelerates the entire hyperdimensional computing (HDC) framework within the memory. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Partial implementations of an HDC framework inside memory can provide considerable performance/energy gains. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint asserts full in-memory acceleration of the entire HDC framework; the publication softens and generalizes this to partial in-memory implementations providing gains, narrowing and tempering the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using language recognition as the use case demonstrates a 7.8x reduction in overall runtime and a 5.3x reduction in energy consumption compared to the FPGA design. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: RTM-based HDC processing demonstrates 7.8× and 5.3× improvements in the overall runtime and energy consumption, respectively, compared to dedicated hardware design realized with an FPGA. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical runtime and energy improvements for the language recognition use case are preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed HDC system reduces energy consumption by 8.6x compared to the state-of-the-art in-memory implementation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed RTM HDC system reduces the energy consumption by 8.6× compared to the state-of-the-art in-memory implementation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported 8.6× energy reduction claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed solution requires minimal additional CMOS circuitry by leveraging a read operation across multiple domains in RTMs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces an additional hardware/implementation detail about minimal CMOS overhead that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper presents a novel strategy called Pareto Adversarial Training for achieving universal adversarial robustness. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The incorporation of the Pareto criterion into adversarial robustness analysis yields a novel strategy called Pareto adversarial training for achieving universal robustness. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both versions present the same novel Pareto adversarial training strategy; wording is effectively equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A comprehensive relationship between natural accuracy, sensitivity-based robustness, and spatial robustness is established. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A comprehensive relationship between natural accuracy, sensitivity-based robustness, and spatial robustness is presented, supported by strong evidence from the perspective of robust representation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the relationship claim but adds that it's supported by strong evidence and the robust representation perspective, which clarifies and strengthens the presentation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of local and global spatial vulnerability into a unified spatial attack and adversarial training approach is proposed. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper investigates the relatively less-explored realm of spatial robustness in adversarial robustness. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The preprint claimed a specific integration of local/global spatial vulnerability into unified attacks/training; the publication presents a more general investigation of spatial robustness, which softens and narrows the original specific-method claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The resulting Pareto front provides an optimal balance between natural accuracy and various adversarial robustness. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The resulting Pareto front provides an optimal balance between natural accuracy and various adversarial robustness. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The Pareto front balance claim is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work is the first to consider universal adversarial robustness via multi-objective optimization. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work is the first to consider universal adversarial robustness via multi-objective optimization. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The novelty claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: No unanimous and non-dictatorial voting rule is strategyproof according to the Gibbard-Satterthwaite theorem. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: No unanimous and non-dictatorial voting rule is strategyproof according to the Gibbard-Satterthwaite theorem. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The theoretical statement is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Several classes of voting rules satisfy the weaker notion of strategyproofness called not obvious manipulability. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Several classes of voting rules satisfy the weaker notion of strategyproofness called not obvious manipulability. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result about classes of rules satisfying the weaker notion is preserved without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Certain voting rules are obviously manipulable when the number of alternatives is relatively large compared to the number of voters. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Certain voting rules are obviously manipulable when the number of alternatives is relatively large compared to the number of voters. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conditional manipulability claim is retained verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Many of the voting rules examined are not obviously manipulable, contrasting with the Gibbard-Satterthwaite theorem. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Many of the voting rules examined are not obviously manipulable, contrasting with the Gibbard-Satterthwaite theorem. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The content is the same, but the publication expresses higher certainty, so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The notion of not obvious manipulability is easier to satisfy than the perfect information assumption of strategyproofness. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The notion of not obvious manipulability is easier to satisfy than the perfect information assumption of strategyproofness. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The comparative claim about ease of satisfying the notion remains unchanged.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A Deep Learning methodology has been developed to classify protein structures. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Artificial Intelligence has been trained to recognize complex biomolecule structures from the Protein Data Bank. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The training/result claim is unchanged and preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Various pre-trained Convolutional Neural Networks, such as InceptionResNetV2 and InceptionV3, were tested to extract significant features for classification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Various pre-trained Convolutional Neural Networks, including InceptionResNetV2 and InceptionV3, have been tested to extract significant features for classification. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental use of specific pretrained CNNs is the same, but the publication reports reduced certainty (high->medium), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A comparative analysis of the performances of the various networks will be produced. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A comparative analysis of the performances of the various networks will be produced. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The forward-looking comparative analysis claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ArSSR model can achieve arbitrary up-sampling rate reconstruction of HR images from any input LR image after training. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ArSSR model can achieve arbitrary and infinite up-sampling rate reconstructions of HR images from any input LR image. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results show that the ArSSR model can achieve state-of-the-art SR performance for 3D HR MR image reconstruction. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The dataset used in this study is made public for future research. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Node-wise localization of GNNs accounts for both global and local aspects of the graph. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method allows each node to be localized into a unique model based on its local context. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.rationale: The statement's text is unchanged but the publication frames it as a limitation rather than a result, which signals a more cautious presentation and thus a slight tempering of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CAMELS project includes 4,233 cosmological simulations, comprising 2,049 N-body and 2,184 hydrodynamic simulations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CAMELS project includes 4233 cosmological simulations, 2049 N-body simulations, and 2184 hydrodynamic simulations. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The numbers and content are the same between preprint and publication, only minor formatting differences (commas/spacing).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CAMELS public data release includes a variety of data products such as halo, subhalo, galaxy, and void catalogues. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The data release contains over 350 terabytes of information, including 143,922 snapshots and millions of halos and galaxies. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The quantitative description of size and contents is retained verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CAMELS-SAM collection combines N-body simulations with the Santa Cruz Semi-Analytic Model. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim corresponds to the statement about CAMELS-SAM combining N-body sims with the Santa Cruz SAM; the publication instead adds other CAMELS-SAM details.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Over 1000 catalogs containing billions of galaxies from CAMELS-SAM are included in the data release. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This specific quantitative claim about CAMELS-SAM catalogs appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Discriminative training of the PLDA parameters gives large gains with respect to the usual generative training. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claims are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed hierarchical approach consistently outperforms the non-hierarchical one for detection of highly related languages. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Direct match; wording and meaning unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The gains from the hierarchical approach are robust to condition mismatch. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The gains from the hierarchical approach are robust to condition mismatch. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same robustness claim appears in the publication without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The complete model is trained discriminatively to optimize a cross-entropy objective. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The complete model is trained discriminatively to optimize a cross-entropy objective. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: ZeroBERTo has better performance for long inputs and shorter execution time compared to existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: ZeroBERTo outperforms XLM-R by about 12% in the F1 score in the FolhaUOL dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new proxy for cluster mass, $Y_\text{conc}$, combines $Y_\text{SZ}$ and concentration of ionized gas ($c_\text{gas}$). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We find a new proxy for cluster mass which combines Y_SZ and concentration of ionized gas: M ∝ Y_conc^(3/5) ≡ Y_SZ^(3/5)(1 - A c_gas). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication gives an explicit scaling relation and formula for Y_conc, clarifying the original statement without altering its core content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: $Y_\text{conc}$ reduces the scatter in the predicted cluster mass $M$ by approximately 20-30% for large clusters compared to using just $Y_\text{SZ}$. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Y_conc reduces the scatter in the predicted M by ∼20 - 30% for large clusters (M ≳ 10^14 h^-1 M⊙), as compared to using just Y_SZ. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit mass threshold defining 'large clusters', narrowing the scope while preserving the quantified scatter reduction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dependence on $c_\text{gas}$ is linked to cores of clusters exhibiting larger scatter than their outskirts. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dependence on c_gas is linked to cores of clusters exhibiting larger scatter than their outskirts. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The explanatory conclusion about c_gas and core scatter is carried over verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: $Y_\text{conc}$ is robust against variations in cosmology, subgrid physics, and cosmic variance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Y_conc is robust against variations in cosmology, subgrid physics, and cosmic variance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Robustness across simulation and cosmological variations is unchanged.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our results and methodology can be useful for accurate multiwavelength cluster mass estimation from upcoming CMB and X-ray surveys. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The stated applicability to future surveys is present and unchanged in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A data-driven approach is proposed to optimize simulation parameters for transferring control policies to a real-world quadrotor. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural network-based policies can operate using only onboard sensor data and run entirely on embedded hardware. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Low-level Pulse-Width Modulated controllers trained with Reinforcement Learning require a more accurate simulation than higher-level control policies. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Learned Indexes are an effective approach to search in a sorted table.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate a general claim about the overall effectiveness of learned indexes; it focuses on search routine choices within learned indexes, so the broad effectiveness claim from the preprint is not preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recent studies indicate that k-ary Search may be better suited than the lower_bound routine for modern computer architectures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: K‐ary search is a better choice than binary search when using learned indexes.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes a more definitive statement aligning k-ary vs binary (lower_bound) in the learned-index context, clarifying and strengthening the preprint's tentative observation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our results provide indications that better choices than the lower_bound routine can be made for the final stage of searching in a Learned Index.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Guidelines for the selection of the Search routine within the learned indexing framework are provided.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the preprint's indications as concrete guidelines for selecting the search routine, clarifying the earlier, more tentative phrasing while keeping the same scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The choice of Search routine may be dependent on the computer architecture used.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The efficiency of search approaches can vary depending on the computer architecture used.
alignments.rationale: Both statements convey the same conclusion that search routine efficiency depends on computer architecture; the publication reiterates this point without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our findings provide new guidelines for the selection of the Search routine within the Learned Indexing framework.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Guidelines for the selection of the Search routine within the learned indexing framework are provided.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the guidelines but with a slightly lower asserted certainty (medium), so the content is maintained though the expressed confidence is somewhat tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed strategies approximate the theoretical distance for samples close to the classification boundary.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed strategies approximate the theoretical distance for samples close to the classification boundary.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach allows formulating an error estimation theory of the approximate distance with respect to the theoretical one.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The obtained results lead to provable robustness guarantees against any adversarial attacks.

alignments.pub_claim_text: No provable results have been presented to estimate and bound the error committed in existing methods.
